name: Diacerein
description: Diacerein is a prodrug which is metabolized to rhein. It is currently approved in France for the treatment of osteoarthritis although the use of diacerein is restricted due to the side effects including sevre diarrhea [L780]. Diacerein is under investigation for the treatment of Insulin Resistance, Diabetes Mellitus (Type 2), and Diabetes-Related Complications.
indication: For the treatment of osteoarthritis affecting the hip or knee [L780].
toxicity: Rhein [DB13174] has an oral LD50 of >5000mg/kg in mice. This is equivalent to a diacerein dose of >6476mg/kg.
mechanismOfAction: Diacerein's active metabolite rhein [DB13174] reduces cartilage destruction by decreasing expression of matrix metalloproteinase (MMP)-1 and -3 as well as upregulating tissue inhibitor of matrix metalloproteinases which serve to reduce the activity of several MMPs [A19339]. The anti-inflammatory action of rhein reduces the level of interleukin-1beta activity which plays a large role in reduction of extracellular matrix production, MMP activity, and continued inflammation [A19338]. Rhein reduces abnormal osteoblast synthetic activity through an unknown mechanism [A19337].
pharmacodynamics: Decreases inflammation and cartilage destruction and also corrects altered osteoblast acitivity [A19337] [A19338] [A19339].

interactions.name: Thioridazine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Thioridazine can be increased when it is combined with Diacerein.

interactions.name: Bosutinib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Bosutinib can be increased when it is combined with Diacerein.

interactions.name: Ibrutinib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Ibrutinib can be increased when it is combined with Diacerein.

interactions.name: Tolvaptan
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Tolvaptan can be increased when it is combined with Diacerein.

interactions.name: Metoprolol
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Metoprolol can be increased when it is combined with Diacerein.

interactions.name: Fesoterodine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of the active metabolites of Fesoterodine can be increased when Fesoterodine is used in combination with Diacerein.

interactions.name: Rilpivirine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Rilpivirine can be increased when it is combined with Diacerein.
